title: Files tab: Stale file detection
description:
Detect potentially stale/orphaned files:
- No git commits in X days (configurable threshold)
- No references from other code files (import analysis)
- Not modified recently
- Possible orphan detection (unreachable code)
Show stale indicator in explorer, filter option, details in panel

title: Tasks tab: Explorer + Details panel redesign
description:
Redesign Tasks tab with explorer/details view:
- Hierarchical view showing task dependencies (parent > children)
- Expandable task groups (by schedule, by type, by status)
- Details panel showing:
  - Task: schedule, last run, duration, dependencies, logs
  - Group: task count, success rate, next scheduled
- Visual dependency tree (what blocks what)
- Filter by status, schedule type, recent failures
Goal: Replace Workflows tab if dependency visualization works here

title: Sitemap Tab: Replace API tab with unified endpoint/URL monitoring
description:
**Goal:** Replace API tab in System Registry with unified Sitemap tab showing ALL discoverable URLs (frontend pages, backend APIs, other services) with real-time health monitoring.

**Key Features:**
- Tree view + Table view toggle for URL browsing
- Hourly health checks (console errors, HTTP status, response time)
- Daily auto-discovery (OpenAPI scan + frontend crawler)
- 7-day health history with cleanup via Status page maintenance section
- Red error badge on tab when issues exist
- Evidence integration for screenshots

**Replaces:** API tab (consolidates api_capabilities data)

---

## Implementation Plan

**Plan file:** `/home/kasadis/.claude/plans/jazzy-roaming-karp.md`

### Database (Phase 1)
- `sitemap_entries` table: port, path, method, entry_type, health_status, console_errors, console_warnings, etc.
- `sitemap_health_history` table: detailed logs with 7-day retention
- Migration: `backend/migrations/119_sitemap_tables.sql`

### Backend Service (Phase 2)
- `sitemap_service.py`: Discovery (OpenAPI, crawler), health checks, CRUD
- Uses existing `console.js` script for console capture

### Backend API (Phase 3)
- GET /api/sitemap/entries (list with filters)
- GET /api/sitemap/entries/{id} (detail)
- POST /api/sitemap/discover (trigger scan)
- POST /api/sitemap/check/{id} (check health)
- GET /api/sitemap/health-summary (aggregate stats)
- POST /api/sitemap/register (manual add)

### Celery Tasks (Phase 4)
- `check_sitemap_health`: Every 1 hour
- `discover_sitemap_entries`: Daily 03:30 UTC
- `cleanup_sitemap_history`: Daily 04:00 UTC (7-day retention)

### Frontend (Phase 5-7)
- `sitemap.ts`: API client
- `SitemapTab.tsx`: Main container with summary cards, view toggle, filters
- `SitemapTreeView.tsx`: Hierarchical tree by port → path
- `SitemapTableView.tsx`: Filterable table with actions
- Replace API tab in `capabilities/page.tsx`
- Add cleanup card to `MaintenanceSection.tsx`

---

## Files to Create
- backend/migrations/119_sitemap_tables.sql
- backend/app/services/sitemap_service.py
- backend/app/api/sitemap.py
- backend/app/tasks/sitemap_tasks.py
- frontend/lib/api/sitemap.ts
- frontend/components/capabilities/SitemapTab.tsx
- frontend/components/capabilities/SitemapTreeView.tsx
- frontend/components/capabilities/SitemapTableView.tsx

## Files to Modify
- backend/app/main.py (register router)
- backend/app/celery_app.py (import tasks)
- backend/app/celery_schedules.py (add schedules)
- frontend/app/capabilities/page.tsx (replace API tab + error badge)
- frontend/components/status/MaintenanceSection.tsx (add cleanup card)

title: Automation Pipeline: Thesis-based watchlist management
description:
**Problem:** Thesis invalidation is implicit (via strategy archival), not explicit. No feedback loop. Watchlist trimming based only on score/time, not thesis validity.

**Changes:**
1. Integrate thesis monitoring into automation pipeline
2. Auto-remove from watchlist when thesis invalidated (unless in portfolio)
3. Link thesis invalidation to Execution Log (transaction history) for validation tracking
4. Daily thesis health check via Celery task
5. Thesis invalidation triggers strategy archival (not the other way around)

**Pipeline Enhancement:**
```
Watchlist Discovery → Thesis Generation → Cross-Validation → Signal Generation
                                ↓
                    Thesis Monitoring (daily)
                                ↓
            Invalidation Detected → Auto-remove OR Flag for Review
                                ↓
                    Execution Log Updated (audit trail)
```

**Files:**
- backend/app/tasks/watchlist_discovery.py - Add thesis-based removal
- backend/app/tasks/thesis_tasks.py (NEW) - Thesis monitoring Celery tasks
- backend/app/config/trading_rules/v1.0.0/rules.yaml - Thesis invalidation rules

**Depends on:** portfolio-ai-rjt (Thesis System)

title: Cleanup: Remove unused narrative generation
description:
**Current State:**
narrative_generator.py generates text that is stored in DB but no longer displayed:
- narrative_headline ('BUY - Price above EMA')
- narrative_action_plan ('BUY around $X, EXIT below $Y')
- narrative_position_sizing ('Buy X shares = $Y invested')  
- narrative_company_health (fundamental bullets)
- narrative_special_notes (earnings warnings, WHY THIS WORKS)

**Problem:**
- 477 lines of code generating text nobody sees
- CPU cost on every watchlist refresh
- Storage cost in database
- Bandwidth cost sending unused data in API responses

**Proposed Changes:**
1. Modify refresh_narrative.py to skip text generation (set to None)
2. Simplify refresh_builders.py to not store narrative fields
3. Keep: signal_type, signal_strength, style_result (still needed)
4. Optional: DB migration to drop narrative columns

**Files:**
- backend/app/watchlist/narrative_generator.py (DELETE or archive)

- backend/app/watchlist/refresh_builders.py (null narrative fields)

title: Add dry_run support to ALL maintenance tasks
description:
CRITICAL: The Dry Run toggle in the Maintenance table was misleading - only 3 tasks supported preview mode, but the UI implied ALL tasks would preview. Tasks that ran without dry_run support actually executed.

## Tasks Needing dry_run Support:
- cleanup_old_logs_task
- cleanup_old_backups_task
- cleanup_old_models_task
- cleanup_solution_state_task
- cleanup_old_agent_runs_task
- cleanup_orphaned_data_task
- cleanup_temp_files_task
- rotate_logs_task
- cleanup_old_news_task (scripts endpoint)
- vacuum_database_task (scripts endpoint)
- validate_integrity (scripts endpoint)

## Implementation Pattern:
1. Add dry_run: bool = False parameter to each task
2. Collect files/records that WOULD be deleted
3. Return details array with preview info when dry_run=True
4. Only execute deletion when dry_run=False
5. Update tasks_router.py to include all tasks in dry_run whitelist
6. Update frontend MaintenanceTable.tsx supportsDryRun flags

## Current State:
- Frontend already skips non-dry_run tasks in preview mode (safe)
- Need backend changes to make preview actually work for all tasks

title: Fix: Recommendations validation_filter logic fails for "both" filter
description:
Error: When validation_filter="both" and validation_type="both", the filter logic incorrectly SKIPS the recommendation instead of showing it.

Location: backend/app/api/recommendations.py:357-363

Current buggy logic:
```python
if (
    validation_filter
    and validation_filter != "all"
    and validation_filter not in (validation_type, "both")
    and not (validation_filter == "both" and validation_type != "both")
):
    continue
```

Test case that fails:
- validation_filter = "both"
- validation_type = "both"
- Expected: SHOW recommendation
- Actual: SKIPS recommendation (bug)

Root cause: Line 360 evaluates to True ("both" not in ("both", "both") is False, but combined logic inverts), causing skip.

Fix needed:
```python
if validation_filter and validation_filter != "all":
    if validation_filter == "both":
        if validation_type != "both":
            continue
    elif validation_type != validation_filter:
        continue
```

Found during: Code review of Watchlist/Picks/Thesis rationalization

title: Maintenance system refactor + file cleanup automation
description:
Complete refactor of Status page maintenance section and implement automated file cleanup.

## Status: Phases 1, 2, 4 DONE - Phase 3 UI Refinement Remaining

### ✅ Phase 1: Comprehensive File Audit (DONE)
Identified cleanup patterns: logs (202MB), backups (172MB), models (9MB), solution_state (20MB)

### ✅ Phase 2: Gitignore Audit (DONE)
.gitignore already correct - issue was cleanup tasks pointing to wrong directories

### ✅ Phase 4: Automated Cleanup Tasks (DONE)
Created 4 new Celery tasks:
- cleanup_old_logs_task (fixed to include backend/logs/)
- cleanup_old_backups_task (keep 5 recent)
- cleanup_old_models_task (keep 3 versions per model)
- cleanup_solution_state_task (keep 14 days)
- All scheduled weekly on Sunday
- API endpoint: GET /api/maintenance/file-cleanup-status

### 🟡 Phase 3: UI Consolidation (REMAINING)

**Current state:** 3 separate cards (MaintenanceCard, FileCleanupCard, MaintenanceStatusCard)

**Goal:** ONE unified maintenance card with consistent formatting:

1. **Consolidate all maintenance items** into single card:
   - File cleanup items (logs, backups, models, solution_state) - DONE, use as template
   - Database maintenance items (cleanup news, vacuum, integrity) - reformat to match
   - System maintenance items from MaintenanceStatusCard - integrate

2. **Consistent item format** (like FileCleanupCard):
   - Icon + Title
   - Size/count metrics
   - Retention policy badge
   - Schedule info
   - Individual run button per item

3. **Master controls:**
   - Master "Run All" button
   - Global Dry Run toggle
   - Downloadable dry-run report (JSON)
   - API endpoint for report: GET /api/maintenance/dry-run-report

4. **Dry-run report features:**
   - Shows what WOULD be deleted/cleaned for each task
   - Accessible via API for Agent Hub review
   - Downloadable for user review before running live

**Success Criteria:**
- Single unified Maintenance card (not 3 separate)
- Consistent visual formatting across all task types
- Dry run toggle with accessible report
- API endpoint for Agent Hub to review dry-run results
notes: Phase 3 UI consolidation DONE: UnifiedMaintenanceCard.tsx created. PAUSED for evidence capture feature (portfolio-ai-d1j completed, portfolio-ai-jm1 created for Screen Capture API). Remaining: dry-run report API, visual verification of maintenance card.

title: Market Event Markers: FOMC, CPI, NFP on sentiment charts
description:
**Problem:** No market-wide event tracking (Fed, CPI, NFP). Catalyst system only tracks company-specific events.

**Event Types to Track (Quality Focus, ~50/year):**
- FOMC rate decisions (8/year) - HIGH IMPACT
- CPI releases (12/year) - HIGH IMPACT
- Non-farm payrolls (12/year) - HIGH IMPACT
- Fed chair speeches (~12/year) - MEDIUM IMPACT
- PCE inflation (12/year) - MEDIUM IMPACT
- GDP releases (4/year) - MEDIUM IMPACT

**NOT including:** Fed speaker comments (~100/year) - too noisy, low signal

**Schema - market_events table:**
- id, event_type (fomc_decision, cpi_release, nfp_release, fed_speech, pce_release, gdp_release)
- event_date, event_time, title
- expected_value, actual_value, surprise_pct
- impact_score (-5 to +5)
- price_change_1h, price_change_1d (SPY correlation)
- source, created_at

**UI Features:**
- Event timeline at top of Market Conditions card
- Hover shows tooltip with event details + impact
- Perpendicular line across all charts when hovering
- Master time selector (1W, 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y) syncs all charts

**Data Source:** Economic calendar (manual seed for 2024-2026, then Celery task to fetch updates)

**Files:**
- backend/app/models/market_events.py (NEW)
- backend/app/services/market_events_service.py (NEW)
- backend/app/tasks/market_events_tasks.py (NEW)
- frontend/components/market/EventTimeline.tsx (NEW)

title: Macro Regime Engine: Fed signals → portfolio actions
description:
**Turn Fed data into actionable portfolio signals**

**1. Regime Classification (backend service):**
- Inputs: Fed Funds rate trend, yield curve slope, balance sheet direction
- Output: regime = Dovish | Neutral | Hawkish
- Sub-signals: curve_status (inverted/flat/steep), liquidity (QE/QT/hold)

**2. Dashboard Indicator (frontend):**
- Fed Stance card: regime + key rates + curve spread
- Color-coded: green (dovish), yellow (neutral), red (hawkish)
- Sparkline of regime changes over time

**3. Sector Tilt Suggestions:**
- Map regime → favored/avoided sectors
- Dovish: Growth, Tech, REITs, Utilities
- Hawkish: Financials, Energy, Value, Cash
- Show on Dashboard + Watchlist page

**4. Watchlist Scoring Adjustment:**
- Regime-aware sector weight in composite score
- Flag holdings misaligned with regime
- Example: High-growth in hawkish regime = warning

**5. Thesis Validation Integration:**
- Parse thesis for rate assumptions
- Auto-flag conflicts: thesis says cuts, Fed is hiking
- Link to thesis validation system

**6. Alert Triggers:**
- Yield curve inversion/uninversion
- Regime change (dovish→hawkish)
- Fed Funds rate change
- Balance sheet >1% weekly change

**Files:**
- backend/app/services/macro_regime_service.py (NEW)
- backend/app/models/macro_regime.py (NEW)
- frontend/components/market/FedStanceCard.tsx (NEW)
- Integrate with: watchlist scoring, thesis system, alerts

title: Agent Hub: Agent should see/reference captured evidence
description:
When evidence is captured in Agent Hub, the agent (Claude/Gemini) cannot see it. The evidence message appears in chat but the agent responds 'I don't have visibility into evidence you've gathered'.

Current state:
- Evidence captured via EvidenceCaptureModal
- Evidence message saved to session (role: 'evidence', metadata: {evidence: {...}})
- Evidence displays in chat UI as clickable card
- But agent context doesn't include evidence data

Needed:
1. When sending messages to agent, include evidence context from recent captures
2. Or inject evidence summary into the conversation context
3. Agent should be able to reference: feature_id, criterion_id, version, console errors, network failures, screenshot URL

Example context injection:
[Evidence captured: DBG-1213-222150/ac-001 v2 - screenshot at /api/artifacts/DBG-1213-222150/ac-001/screenshot - 0 console errors, 0 network failures]

Files:
- frontend/components/agents/AgentPanel.tsx (message sending)
- services/dev-companion/dev_companion/server.py (context handling)

title: Sitemap: Comprehensive discovery for all service types
description:
**Problem:** Current sitemap discovery only covers:
- Backend API endpoints (via OpenAPI on port 8000)
- Frontend pages (via link crawling on port 3000)

**Missing from sitemap:**
- WebSocket endpoints (port 9999 dev-companion)
- Redis endpoints (port 6379)
- Any future services not using OpenAPI/HTML

**Goal:** Holistic sitemap that captures ALL discoverable endpoints across all service types.

**Implementation:**

1. **WebSocket Discovery:**
   - Probe discovered ports for WebSocket upgrade capability
   - Register WS endpoints with entry_type='websocket'
   - Parse dev-companion routes if available

2. **Service-Type-Aware Discovery:**
   - Use PortDiscovery service_type hints
   - Apply appropriate discovery method per type:
     - backend → OpenAPI
     - frontend → crawl + Next.js app dir parsing
     - websocket → WS probe + manual routes
     - cache (redis) → skip or mark as internal

3. **Unified Discovery Runner:**
   - Enhance run_discovery() to iterate all discovered ports
   - Apply type-appropriate discovery for each
   - Handle graceful fallbacks (probe if no specific method)

4. **Manual Registration for Non-Discoverable:**
   - Add seed data for known endpoints without auto-discovery
   - Document how to register custom endpoints

**Files:**
- backend/app/services/sitemap_service.py - Add discover_websocket_endpoints(), enhance run_discovery()
- backend/app/api/sitemap.py - Expose new discovery methods

**Depends on:** portfolio-ai-9op (port discovery - DONE), portfolio-ai-81g (Next.js routes - in progress)

title: Maintenance system refactor + file cleanup automation
description:
Complete refactor of Status page maintenance section and implement automated file cleanup.

**DEPENDS ON:** portfolio-ai-uep (schema-aware maintenance) - this provides the foundation for dynamic, self-healing maintenance tasks.

## Phase 1: Comprehensive File Audit
Thorough exploration of ALL files to identify patterns that should be cleaned up regularly:
- Log files (backend/logs/*.log, rotated logs)
- Backup files (backups/*.sql, old pre-migration backups)
- Model artifacts (backend/models/*.joblib - which are needed vs stale?)
- Cache files, temp files, build artifacts
- Any other accumulated cruft

**Deliverable:** Document file patterns and retention policies

## Phase 2: Gitignore Audit
Review and update .gitignore:
- Ensure all artifact patterns are properly ignored
- Prevent future accumulation of files that shouldn't be tracked
- Document what's ignored and why

## Phase 3: Status Page Maintenance Refactor
Improve maintenance UI and functionality:
- Show file cleanup status (logs, backups, artifacts)
- Add disk space monitoring per category
- Implement one-click cleanup actions
- Show cleanup history/stats

## Phase 4: Automated Cleanup Tasks
Create Celery tasks for regular cleanup:
- Log rotation/cleanup (keep N days)
- Backup pruning (keep N recent)
- Stale model artifact cleanup
- Temp file cleanup

**Success Criteria:**
- Maintenance page shows complete system health
- Automated tasks keep disk usage in check
- No manual intervention needed for routine cleanup

title: Agent Hub: Connect to Chrome DevTools MCP
description:
Wire the Agent Hub (dev-companion backend) to use Chrome DevTools MCP tools:

Current state:
- Chrome DevTools MCP is configured in Claude Code
- Agent Hub uses its own WebSocket connection to dev-companion
- MCP tools are not directly accessible from Agent Hub

Required:
1. Expose Chrome DevTools MCP tools through dev-companion API
2. Allow agents to call screenshot/click/navigate from Agent Hub
3. Forward tool calls and results between Agent Hub and MCP

Architecture options:
A) dev-companion calls MCP server directly
B) dev-companion calls Chrome DevTools Protocol directly (skip MCP)
C) Agent Hub calls MCP via a bridge API

This is the foundation task - must be done before screenshot/interaction beads can work.

title: Fix: Paper trades not updating prices (ZeroDivisionError + DRY violation)
description:
**Root Cause:** Bad AAPL trade with entry_price=0.0 crashes calculate_trade_return() with ZeroDivisionError. Entire daily update task fails, preventing ALL open trades from updating.

**Evidence:** 13 open trades show entry_price = current_price for 3-11 days. Only NVDA/AMD have non-zero P&L (updated before crash).

**DRY Violation:** Separate update_paper_trades_task duplicates price fetching when watchlist already maintains price_cache.

**Fix Required:**
1. Add zero-check guard in calculate_trade_return() before division
2. Delete/fix bad AAPL trade (idea_id: 2ded9149-332e-4031-a79a-e764039c08ae)
3. Add DB constraint: CHECK (entry_price > 0)
4. Refactor to use shared price_cache from watchlist (DRY)
5. P&L calculates dynamically from cached prices

**Files:**
- backend/app/analytics/paper_trading_portfolio.py
- backend/app/api/paper_trading.py
- backend/app/tasks/agent_tasks.py
notes: Reopened: Need to implement dynamic P&L calculation from price_cache, not just fix ZeroDivisionError

Deferred due to context limits, not because it shouldn't be done. NEXT SESSION: Tackle with full context, run TypeScript build after changes to catch all usages.

title: Refactor: Dedicated risk assessment module
description:
**Current State:**
Risk is a byproduct of style classification in signal_classifier.py:classify_trading_style().
It uses simple heuristic mapping (Index→Low, Event→High, etc.) rather than actual risk metrics.

**Problems:**
1. Risk coupled to Style - not independent analysis
2. Heuristic-based - no real risk metrics (ATR, beta, volatility)
3. Static mapping - same RSI zone always gives same risk
4. No portfolio context - ignores concentration/correlation

**Proposed Changes:**
1. Create dedicated `risk_calculator.py` with:
   - Volatility-based risk (ATR, historical std dev)
   - Beta-relative risk (vs SPY)
   - Earnings proximity risk boost
   - VIX-adjusted market risk
2. Extract risk logic from classify_trading_style()
3. Keep style classification separate (style \!= risk)
4. Update refresh pipeline to call risk calculator independently

**Files:**
- NEW: backend/app/watchlist/risk_calculator.py
- MODIFY: backend/app/watchlist/signal_classifier.py (remove risk from style)
- MODIFY: backend/app/watchlist/refresh_narrative.py (call risk separately)
- MODIFY: frontend display if risk format changes

**Dependencies:** None - can be done independently

title: Evidence capture: Handle pages with internal scrolling
description:
The capture-evidence.js script only captures viewport-sized screenshots (1280x720) when pages have internal scrolling containers (like watchlist table).

Problem:
- document.documentElement.scrollHeight returns viewport height (720px)
- Content inside overflow:auto containers isn't captured
- User sees truncated screenshots (cuts off after first few rows)

Solution needed:
1. Detect scrollable containers (overflow: auto/scroll)
2. Temporarily expand them (height: auto, maxHeight: none, overflow: visible)
3. Recalculate page dimensions
4. Take full-page screenshot
5. Restore original styles

Files:
- .claude/skills/browser-automation/scripts/capture-evidence.js

Test with: /watchlist page which has scrollable table

title: Thesis: Web search enrichment + data conflict detection
description:
**Problem:** Thesis value drivers (market size, company position, upside potential) could be enriched with web search. Additionally, conflicts between web search data and our intelligence API data could alert to stale/incorrect data.

**Enhancement:**
1. Optional web search enrichment during thesis generation
2. Conflict detection between web search results and intelligence API data
3. Alert generation when significant conflicts are detected
4. Data quality improvement signal

**Conflict Examples:**
- Web says 'earnings beat' but our catalyst data shows miss
- Web mentions CEO resignation but we have no catalyst
- Web shows different price/market cap than our data
- Web sentiment diverges significantly from our news sentiment

**Scope Control (to prevent agent spiraling):**
- Web search ONLY called during explicit thesis generation, NOT on every refresh
- Rate limited: 1 web search per symbol per day maximum
- Token budget: max 2000 tokens per web search call
- Result caching: 24h cache to prevent redundant calls
- Threshold-based: Only alert on >30% data divergence

**Files:**
- backend/app/services/thesis_service.py - Add web search enrichment method
- backend/app/services/conflict_detection.py (NEW) - Compare web vs API data
- backend/app/models/thesis.py - Add web_search_enrichment field

**Depends on:** portfolio-ai-rjt (Thesis System), portfolio-ai-cr1 (SerpAPI)

title: Historical Earnings/Catalyst Data for Event-Driven Backtesting
description:
**Problem:** Current backtesting is TECHNICAL ONLY (RSI, MACD, price action). It cannot validate thesis claims about earnings, catalysts, or fundamentals.

**Gap:** Cannot answer questions like:
- 'What happened last time this stock missed earnings?'
- 'How does this stock react to sector-wide selloffs?'
- 'What's the historical volatility around catalysts?'

**Solution:** Backfill historical event data to enable true event-driven backtesting.

**Data Needed:**
1. Historical earnings dates + actual vs expected (EPS, revenue)
2. Historical earnings reactions (price change 1d, 5d, 30d after)
3. Historical catalyst events (product launches, FDA approvals, etc.)
4. Corporate actions (splits, dividends)

**Data Sources:**
- Yahoo Finance (free, historical earnings)
- Alpha Vantage (free tier, earnings calendar)
- SEC EDGAR (8-K filings for material events)

**Schema Addition:**
- earnings_history table: symbol, date, eps_expected, eps_actual, revenue_expected, revenue_actual, surprise_pct, price_reaction_1d, price_reaction_5d

**Files:**
- backend/app/models/earnings.py (NEW)
- backend/app/tasks/earnings_backfill.py (NEW)
- backend/app/backtest/event_driven.py (NEW)

**Depends on:** portfolio-ai-rft (architecture rationalization first)

title: Thesis-Macro Integration: Rate assumptions + conflict detection
description:
**Extend thesis system with macro awareness**

**1. Thesis Macro Assumptions (new fields):**
- rate_assumption: expects_cuts | expects_hikes | expects_hold | rate_agnostic
- rate_sensitivity: high | medium | low (how much rates affect this thesis)
- regime_alignment: dovish | hawkish | neutral | any

**2. Automated Conflict Detection:**
- Compare thesis.rate_assumption vs current Fed regime
- Example: Thesis says 'expects_cuts' but regime is 'hawkish' → CONFLICT
- Add to check_invalidation_triggers(): macro_conflict check

**3. Thesis Generation Enhancement:**
- LLM prompt includes current Fed stance when generating thesis
- Asks: 'Is this thesis rate-sensitive? What rate environment does it assume?'
- Stores assumptions in thesis metadata

**4. Alert Generation:**
- On regime change: scan all active theses for conflicts
- Create thesis_conflict alert: 'NVDA thesis assumes rate cuts, but Fed turned hawkish'
- Link alert to thesis detail page

**5. UI Updates:**
- Thesis detail: show macro assumptions section
- Conflict badge on thesis if misaligned with current regime
- Recommendation adjustment: 'Thesis valid but regime headwind'

**Files:**
- backend/app/models/thesis.py (add macro fields)
- backend/app/services/thesis_service.py (conflict detection)
- backend/app/tasks/thesis_monitoring_tasks.py (regime change scan)
- frontend thesis components (assumption display)

**Depends on:** Alert Infrastructure (722), Macro Regime Engine (6lt)

title: Viewport capture: Use Screen Capture API for true pixel capture
description:
Current dom-to-image approach doesn't capture scroll position. Need to use Screen Capture API (getDisplayMedia).

## Problem
DOM-to-image libraries (html2canvas, dom-to-image-more, modern-screenshot) render DOM tree from scratch - they don't see scroll position or interactive state.

## Solution
Use browser's Screen Capture API:
- getDisplayMedia() captures actual screen pixels
- Preserves scroll position, expanded sections, everything
- One-click permission popup (unavoidable without enterprise policy)

## Implementation
1. Update EvidenceCaptureModal viewport mode to use getDisplayMedia
2. Capture video frame → convert to canvas → base64 PNG
3. Send to existing /api/artifacts/viewport-capture endpoint

## Code pattern
```javascript
const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia({ 
  video: { displaySurface: 'browser' },
  preferCurrentTab: true 
});
const track = stream.getVideoTracks()[0];
const imageCapture = new ImageCapture(track);
const bitmap = await imageCapture.grabFrame();
// Convert bitmap to canvas to base64
```

## Files to modify
- frontend/components/agents/EvidenceCaptureModal.tsx (viewport capture function)

## Current state
- Backend endpoint ready: POST /api/artifacts/viewport-capture
- Modal UI ready with Viewport mode
- Just need to swap dom-to-image for Screen Capture API

title: Rationalize Watchlist/Picks/Thesis Architecture
description:
**Problem:** Watchlist shows BUY signals + trade levels BEFORE due diligence. Multiple redundant 'WHY' systems. Confused flow between Watchlist and Picks.

**Solution:** Remove premature signals from Watchlist, implement Either/Or gating for Picks.

**Key Changes:**

1. **Remove from Watchlist expanded row:**
   - SignalStyleRow (BUY badge, Style, Hold, Risk)
   - TradeLevels (Entry/Stop/Target)
   - PositionSizing (Shares/Investment)
   - WhyThisWorks
   - Rename 'Trading Intelligence' → 'Symbol Context'

2. **Delete dead code:**
   - backend/app/watchlist/narrative_generator.py (477 lines)
   - buildWhyThisWorksCopy() in ExpandedRowNarrative.tsx

3. **Add progression indicators to Watchlist:**
   - Thesis status badge: None | Validated | Invalidated
   - Strategy status badge: None | Backtested | Active

4. **Gate Picks with Either/Or logic:**
   - Thesis validated (CV ≥0.7) OR Backtest passed (Sharpe ≥1.0)
   - Add validation_type field: 'thesis' | 'backtest' | 'both'
   - Validation badge: 📝 Thesis | 📊 Technical | ⭐ Both

**Files:**
- frontend/components/watchlist/ExpandedRowNarrative.tsx (-150 lines)

title: Thesis System: Structured, tracked, cross-validated investment thesis
description:
**Problem:** Current 'Why this works' is generated narrative, not a tracked/validated thesis. No version tracking, no explicit invalidation, no cross-validation.

**New Thesis System:**
1. Structured data model (core_reasons, key_catalysts, risks, expected_return, value_drivers)
2. Cross-validation with Claude AND Gemini using intelligence API data as guardrails
3. Version tracking (thesis history preserved)
4. Explicit invalidation triggers with configurable auto-remove vs flag-for-review
5. Value drivers (AI research) merged into thesis, cached until invalidation

**Schema - thesis table:**
- id, symbol, version, status (active/invalidated/superseded)
- action (BUY/HOLD/SELL), core_reasons (JSONB), key_catalysts (JSONB)
- risks (JSONB), expected_return_pct, expected_timeframe_days
- value_drivers (JSONB) - market sizing, company position, upside potential
- claude_validation (JSONB), gemini_validation (JSONB), cross_validation_score
- invalidation_reason, invalidated_at, timestamps

**Invalidation Triggers:**
- Price drops below stop loss
- Key catalyst fails to materialize
- Fundamental deterioration (earnings miss, guidance cut)
- Cross-validation score drops below threshold

**Settings Addition:** Auto-remove toggle (auto-remove vs flag-for-review)

**Files:**
- backend/app/models/thesis.py (NEW)
- backend/app/services/thesis_service.py (NEW)
- backend/app/api/thesis.py (NEW)
- frontend/components/watchlist/expanded/ThesisSection.tsx (NEW)
- backend/app/config/trading_rules/v1.0.0/rules.yaml

title: Schema-aware maintenance system (self-healing)
description:
Refactor maintenance tasks to use db_capabilities instead of hardcoded table names.

**Problem:**
- Maintenance tasks hardcode table names (news_headlines, agent_ideas, ws.symbol)
- When schema changes, maintenance breaks silently or with cryptic errors
- No single source of truth for what tables exist and their cleanup rules

**Solution:**
- Query db_capabilities for table metadata at runtime
- Define cleanup rules in db_capabilities (retention_days, cleanup_strategy)
- Maintenance tasks iterate over tables with cleanup rules defined

**Scope:**
- Update cleanup_old_news.py to use db_capabilities
- Update any other maintenance scripts with hardcoded table names
- Add retention metadata to db_capabilities schema

**NEXT:** After completing this, proceed to portfolio-ai-8ba (Maintenance system refactor + file cleanup automation) which depends on this schema-aware foundation.
